reviews.review_description: A big Problem is that you can overwrite your Save on accident. Happened to me
Good game
Cool

It's frustrating seeing a game with so much potential been wasted. Creating dungeons is really fun. Playing them sucks. Here's how to fix the gameplay easily.
1. Add a insta death option to add tension. People literally walk through traps loosing little health.
2. Add an option where clipping through walls kills instantly. Seems walking through walls to avoid damage is possible for now.
3. Make enemies more aggressive.
Three minor changes that will add so much more fun to the gameplay.

There are many others like multiplayer, downloading dungeons etc but I guess those require much more resources. The 3 I mentioned are just tweaks but they'll really elevate the experience.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! I could definitely add an insta-death game mode, so you can decide from the start how you want to play. I've added it to my to-do list!

reviews.review_description: This game is fun

reviews.review_title: Cannot play glitch, and y-axis idea.
reviews.review_description: Like many people who already reported this bug I cannot start. And as the person before me who voted 3 stars (garyphod) because the game is great and because of this one bug it loses 2 stars, I agree. I do not know if my room is perhaps too small for the game to accept or if I put something in an "impossible place" but since the devs already know of this glitch I will stop complaining about it. But instead of that I will say; I wish I could lift the key for the door on top of objects. By that I mean I want to be able to change its y-axis. If I could, I could hide it. -0 stars which is because it is such a small detail that it is hardly a problem just an idea. Like maybe if the player presses down on the stick and moves up it will go up the y-axis? Also question: how do I change the room layout? Like I used to have a shelf but I moved it and now I have a random Grey box there in-game but the thing it's warning me abt isn't there. Also, I like that the dev of this game actually respond to critiques with open minds and try to actually improve the game rather than never reading the reviews!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the detailed report! I've now patch the start issue, it was related to saving. I've noted down your idea for the key, it's a good one for sure! As for your room layout, that's done in in your Meta Quest settings. You'll need to create a new dungeon for your new layout though, as previous dungeons will be mapped to the previous rooms ID.

reviews.review_description: I wish we didn't have to walk in real life.

Seems like a super interesting VR game, I am 100% involved in it but how do I erase and create new rooms? I’ve tried deleting the game, I’ve tried erasing the guardian save data from the quest 2 but still nothing. Is there any way to erase the “meta rooms” and if so, how??

developer_response.body: If you are talking about your meta rooms and not the dungeons, that's done on in your Meta Settings and not in-game. If you open Meta Quest menu it's under "Settings > Environment Setup > Space Setup > Manage Your Spaces". Select "Clear" and it'll clear all your rooms. You can also see underneath a list of all the rooms so you can delete them individually.

reviews.review_description:
I bought this game two years ago when I got my quest 2.
I recently bought a quest 3 and have revisited dungeon maker. It has improved in so many ways I felt the desire to recommend it.
It's fun and funny to watch others hop around in the living room. 
Although it's a thriller it is not scary. 
It would make a great gift this Christmas and the extra add-ons too ...

One of the best mixed reality vr games, super immersive and fun to traverse in real life through a dungeon. It is also fun for family, kids, and parents since there is little motion sickness and simple gameplay. I am so glad that this game is free, because this is one of the best and most replayable free vr games yet.

reviews.review_title: Best mixed reality game I've ever played
reviews.review_description:
Dungeon Maker appeals to me because it adds a level of believability to the game by having real world boundaries that correspond to the virtual boundaries created by the game.  That alone adds a level of realism to the experience that even my favorite VR games are missing. By having the ability to repaint every stationary object in the room you get a VR experience like none other. Notwithstanding you can place virtual walls and traps anywhere you like, it's just instead of being limited to a small area in my house I can utilize the entire house!
     I'll admit building my own dungeons can get tedious at times.  But the end result is totally worth the effort.  The portal feature allows you to remap the same room with different traps, treasures, and enemies. Great game if you have the time to invest in the set up.

reviews.review_title: Finally - An App I'd Pay For!
reviews.review_description:
I remember when Duke Nukem introduced the map making option and I'd spend hours making custom maps to roam around in 3D. Now you can do that in VR! It's tedious and time consuming, but oh so glorious to see other people tackle the traps you lay out. 

The potential of this game is immense. My kids love going through the mazes I build and I love making them. Yes it has bugs
but it is App Labs after all and the concept is so cool. It's so great that the developer is constantly working to improve the game as well. 


We need more apps like this that are totally based on the physical space you're in rather than needing to teleport. 
I'd gladly pay for this app when it's all fixed up.

developer_response.body: Hi. Thanks for the review! It seemsthat you may be on an old version of the app. The thumbstick selections menu was removed a very long time ago ^^. Please make sure to update to the latest version, I've been pushing updates twice a week for a long time now, so there's been a lot of progress. I'd love to get more feedback and ideas from you, join the official Discord and we can chat directly: http://www.dungeon-maker.com/

reviews.review_description:
With so many games out there, it’s tough to stand out, but Dungeon Maker totally nails it. It lets you create adventures that are as wild as your imagination and the space available your room. The game gives you tools to build actual dungeons with traps like swinging axes and spinning blades, plus enemies like skeletons that you can set to patrol wherever you want.

With all the other tools they give you, you can turn your room into something straight out of a '90s style dungeon crawler except with VR, it’s like you’re actually in the game.  It might sound cheesy on paper, but trust me, walking across a wooden plank over a spiked floor, with lava pools on both sides and traps everywhere, is a seriously awesome brain meltdown. And they’re always adding cool new features and tools, so it just keeps getting better.

In addition, the developer has a Discord server where you can ask questions or get help, and they respond with direct feedback.
awesome idea, great for showing people what mixed reality can do.

One of the best free game if you have space
Need a fonction for edit outside of the room, sit down

I enjoyed making different dungeons but the skeletons keep spawning in different random places and are often seen walking through the floor or ceiling. And unless you can pick up a sword to attack them I see very little point in them at all. Would be better to give us more things like the swinging axes and so forth, maybe an intermittent flamethrower or fall away flooring or something.

Anyway, it's a good concept and not a bad first game but I'd like to see more updates flood into this game or if need be a second game. 

*Edit* Thanks for the response! Glad to hear a proper dev who cares about the game and community

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing and for the review. This game is still very much a work in progress, I've been pushing updates twice a week for a while (check out the store blog posts for info), and have so much more to come! The skeletons need to be placed correctly in your space: for the patrol use the two black spheres on the floor to set the patrol points. For the guard, use the black sphere on the floor to expand the guard area. I've been designing more traps, v0.39 added the Slicer and in the next updates you'll see more coming in. Thanks again for the support! Do join the Discord to share your thoughts with me directly, half the content that goes into the game comes from the community feedback. https://discord.gg/4aBkQYd6nR

reviews.review_title: Having a blast / Je m'amuse comme un fou
reviews.review_description:
I've discovered Dungeon Maker when I bought my Quest 2 in April 2022.  Since then, I've been using my home to showcase the Quest 2, using Dungeon Maker.  People are impressed by what we can do to turn our own home into something so amazing.  I've deleted my levels (we moved since) and am working on a new multi level dungeon in my basement.  Give this free game a try - you'll love it!
-----------------------
J'ai découvert Dungeon Maker lorsque j'ai acheté ma Quest 2 en avril 2022.  Depuis, j'utilise ma maison pour présenter le Quest 2, en utilisant Dungeon Maker.  Les gens sont impressionnés par ce que nous pouvons faire pour transformer notre propre maison en quelque chose d'aussi incroyable.  J'ai supprimé mes niveaux (nous avons déménagé depuis) et je travaille sur un nouveau donjon à plusieurs niveaux dans mon sous-sol.  Essayez ce jeu gratuit - vous l'adorerez !

The developer is continuing to improve the experience. This is one that is already fun, but also shows lots of promise. Currently getting regular updates, with new features being added; this project is coming along nicely. At the current price of nothing it's hard to pass up if your looking for a novel mixed reality experience.

reviews.review_description:
I spent half a day without putting down headset creating 3 stage dungeon across three rooms for my daughter and saved it, but when i put down my headset for a minute and then put it back on and loaded dungeon, all three was turned cca 90 degrees off, therefore became unplayable, it happened 2 times.
You can imagine my feelings after this disapointment.
Would give one star, but i had fun creating those dungeons. I have no idea why is this happening.

developer_response.body: Hey! Sorry this happened to you, but fear not Sire! If you saved them, they are still there. You just need to re-orient them the correct way. You can actually do that manually, in the menu there is a tool called MOVE ALL. Use that to move/rotate your dungeon back to it's original position.  :)

reviews.review_title: Unique Experience With Lots Of Potential
reviews.review_description:
Had a great time making my own dungeon and playing it with some help from my son. A lot of potential here, looking forward to seeing where this one goes!

Find my gameplay video here: https://youtu.be/lndnWHh_cqw

Or just search 'VR Reviews' in YouTube and my channel should come up there.

This is a great idea and has lots of potential. I am not sure how to move individual items once they have been placed in the scene. Placing them feels pretty random, so it would be better if you could either move drop them where you want after selecting them from the menu, or edit their position after you have placed them. 
Otherwise, a great start and I will keep my eye on this title.

developer_response.body:
Sire! Glad you are enjoying it! As for item placement, you can absolutely move everything around as you like. The main gameplay instruction are on the instructions pillar directly in front of you the first time you start, if you deleted the pillar you can bring it back with the HOW TO button in the menu. 

I'll also explain here how it works: once you've selected an item in the menu it'll appear in front of you in your space. Then point the controller laser at that item, grip the hand trigger on your controller to grab it and then move it wherever you like by moving your controller. To rotate the item simply use the joystick while you have it grabbed.

Good luck adventurer!
